correction.rationale: The mask matches all semantic, anatomical, and spatial requirements perfectly.

judgments.evidence: The mask perfectly conforms to the literal 'foot_mtp' boundary rule, which excludes the toes starting from the metatarsophalangeal line. Side assignment is correct as the character's right foot.

observations.contour: The white contour line is clean and conforms to the foot's shape and the straight cut at the ankle boundary.
observations.full_context: The character is standing in profile, and the crop focuses on their right foot, which is placed flat on the floor in the foreground.
observations.mask: The mask represents a single component covering the foot base up to the ankle, excluding the toes.
observations.neighbor_overlap: No neighboring part overlap or conflict is present.
observations.overlay: The red overlay accurately covers the foot base and terminates precisely at the metatarsophalangeal line as required by the ontology.
observations.source_crop: High-quality image showing the entire right foot with clearly visible metatarsophalangeal joint area.

correction.rationale: Use SAM refinement with positives across the ankle-to-dorsal-foot base and negatives on the lower-leg shaft, all visible toes, and background. The MTP exclusion is safer with negative points placed over the toe region than with a coarse polygon.

judgments.evidence: The candidate side is correct, but the mask includes visible lower-leg area up to the crop boundary. For right_foot_base, the mask must cover the visible foot proximal to the MTP line while excluding toes; this proposal is anatomically too loose proximally and has a missing/incorrect ankle boundary.

observations.contour: The cyan contour follows the foot's outer silhouette reasonably on the lateral and plantar edges, but its upper boundary is clipped at the crop top and its distal boundary is a coarse straight division that does not reliably trace the MTP line.
observations.full_context: The candidate is the character's right foot according to the supplied semantic pose-chain evidence. The visible right foot is the forward foot, with toes extending toward the viewer-right.
observations.mask: The mask is a single component but extends from the top edge of the crop down across the ankle and dorsal foot. It includes substantial lower-leg area and stops at an approximately vertical line before the toes, so it is not a correct foot_mtp mask.
observations.neighbor_overlap: The mask does not substantially include the toes, but it overlaps the neighboring lower-leg/ankle region and may touch the adjacent rear foot near the left edge.
observations.overlay: The red overlay visibly covers the lower-leg shaft from the crop's top edge and most of the foot dorsum, while excluding the toes. The top edge is an artificial crop-edge boundary rather than the visible ankle boundary.
observations.source_crop: The crop clearly shows the ankle, dorsal foot, and toes. The anatomical foot-base region ends at the metatarsophalangeal line before the toes; the upper crop contains lower-leg/ankle area that should not be included without a visible foot boundary.

correction.rationale: Mask boundary and side assignment are correct for this instance; no correction needed despite unrelated systemic QC flags on other body parts.

judgments.evidence: The right_foot_base mask captures ankle-to-ball coverage of the correct (right) foot per pose-chain evidence, cleanly excludes the toes at the MTP line, and shows no clothing/background contamination in this crop.

observations.contour: White contour follows the ankle top edge, side ridges of the foot, and heel bottom edge accurately, with the diagonal cutoff at the MTP line before the toes.
observations.full_context: Cyan ROI box correctly brackets the forward-most (right) foot near the base of the leg; pose-chain distances confirm this is the right foot (178px vs 692px to left chain).
observations.mask: Binary mask covers the ankle down through the heel and ball of foot, terminating in a diagonal edge that stops before the toe segments, consistent with foot_mtp boundary rule.
observations.neighbor_overlap: No overlap with toes region (kept separate) and no bleed onto the other foot/leg in the background of the crop; left foot in background is excluded.
observations.overlay: Red overlay aligns tightly with the visible foot-base skin (ankle, dorsum, heel, ball) and does not bleed onto the separate toes or the background floor.
observations.source_crop: High-res crop shows ankle, dorsum, heel, and ball of foot with toes extending to the right; a natural crease/shadow line marks the MTP boundary before the toes.
